name: Marine War Risk Insurance
summary: Day 67 Tuesday. **AWRP RE-SPIKE RISK ON PROJECT FREEDOM ANNOUNCEMENT** — May 4 Iran kinetic restart + US Project Freedom armed-escort operation + SK HMM Namu damaged in Hormuz = new pricing event for war risk insurance market. Current rates per S&P Global / Lloyd's List / Argus: 0.8-1.5% AWRP (off March 5-10% peak) for Hormuz transits, 8x pre-war 0.1-0.15% baseline. Insurance market had been showing 'cautious stabilization' through mid-April — May 4 events likely re-spike. Joint War Committee Persian Gulf 'high-risk' designation MAINTAINED. War risk insurance is LAGGING INDICATOR but Project Freedom is a STRUCTURAL US institutional commitment to de-recognize Iran's blockade — re-pricing on this scale typically takes 3-7 sessions to manifest. The watch trigger: AWRP at 5%+ within 5 sessions confirms regime re-elevation. Watch for: P&I Club statements on May 5-7; insurers may temporarily WITHDRAW cover (as happened March 3 in initial crisis) before reinsurance chain rebuilt. SK HMM Namu damage = first non-belligerent-flag commercial-fleet damage of the crisis; expands the 'who is at risk' framing beyond US/UK/Israeli flags to Korean/Panama-flagged. 329+ vessels in Persian Gulf coverage gap framework persists.

current.cover_current: $10-14M per Persian Gulf voyage; broad rates 0.8-1.5%; US/UK/Israeli flags 3x; some easing to 0.35-0.45% on broader MEG routes. 329 vessels = ~$352B coverage gap.

active_triggers.condition: Lloyd's JWC removes Arabian Gulf from conflict zone designation
